zh.North Church, Errol, Perthshire, Scotlan: 英國蘇格蘭培爾埃羅爾諾斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Duncane於1526年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾埃羅爾諾斯 邱奇。她於1548年與Richard Davie在英國蘇格蘭培爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1582年在英國蘇格蘭培爾享年56歲。

nameMeanings.name: Duncane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: from the Older Scots personal name Dunecan, itself from the traditional Irish royal name Donnchad(h), derived from donn ‘brown-haired’ + cath ‘battle’. Judging by the Scots form, the Scottish Gaelic intermediary seems to have been understood as containing ceann ‘head’, as if the whole name meant ‘brown head’; compare sense 2. In Ireland the name was Anglicized as Donagh or Donaghue. Compare Donahue .
Irish: used as an Anglicized equivalent of Gaelic Ó Duinnchinn ‘descendant of Donncheann’, a byname composed of the elements donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’ + ceann ‘head’.

variants.name: Duncanson
meanings: Scottish: from the Scottish personal name Duncan + son ‘son of Duncan’.

variants.name: Dinkin
meanings: Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metronymic, with the addition of the Slavic possessive suffix -in, from the Yiddish female personal name Dinke, a hypocoristic form of Biblical Dinah, from Hebrew dina ‘judged, vindicated’.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinnchinn (see Duncan 2).

variants.name: Donkin
meanings: English (Durham and Northumberland): northeastern English variant of Scottish   Duncan  .

variants.name: Donahue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Aidan, Siobhan, Aileen, Conan, Fidelma, John Patrick, Kieran, Parnell.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Donnchadha ‘descendant of Donnchadh’, a personal name (sometimes Anglicized as Duncan in Scotland), composed of the elements donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’ + cath ‘battle’.

variants.name: Dunkin
meanings: Irish (Fermanagh): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinnchinn (see Duncan ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Duncane was born in 1526, in North Church, Errol, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. She married Richard Davie in 1548, in Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1582, in Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 56.

ko.North Church, Errol, Perthshire, Scotlan: 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 에럴 노르스 c후르치
ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Duncane(은)는 1526년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 에럴 노르스 c후르치에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1548년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르에서 Richard Davie(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1582년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.
